conversations_memory:
**Work context**

Daniel Jones is the Director of Operations (transitioning to COO) at Maisy Railing, a custom aluminum railing manufacturer based in Hayden, ID, where he started on 2/9/2026. His role centers on driving a comprehensive manufacturing automation roadmap across CNC machining, robotic welding, and powder coating, with a mandate for near-autonomous manufacturing and goals of 60–75% labor cost reduction and 2–3x capacity increases. Key colleagues include Rocky and Anthony (owners/decision-makers), Amber (Operations Lead), Kyle (VP of Sales, transitioning to VP of Business Development), Adam (Marketing Manager), and shop staff including Michael, Jace, and Nick.

**Personal context**

Daniel lives in Sandpoint, ID and does remodeling/contracting work as a side project. He communicates directly and informally, uses phrases like "brother," and holds himself to high professional standards — he strives to produce corporate-quality work and has a strong drive for career advancement.

**Top of mind**

Daniel is actively troubleshooting and operationalizing the Laguna Swift CNC Router (Maisy Railing's machine), including homing fault diagnosis, parameter correction, and first production runs of aluminum post drilling in Fusion 360 CAM. He is also in an extended development sprint on the custom React/Vite ERP (maisy-erp.vercel.app), recently adding a Process Cost Analysis page, BOM engine improvements, Queue Analyzer persistence, a new `shopqueue` role, and a redesigned login screen. A side contracting job (bathroom remodel for clients in Sagle, ID) is also active, with a labor-only contract recently finalized.

**Brief history**

*Recent months*

ERP development has been the dominant workstream, with continuous feature expansion across orders, production, inventory, shipping, invoicing, commissions, QuickBooks integration, OneDrive order import, and financial reporting — all deployed via GitHub/Vercel (repo: Mr-Jones01/maisy-erp, storage key: maisy_erp_v4). The deploy workflow is always: `cp ~/Downloads/App.jsx ~/Documents/maisy-erp/src/App.jsx && git add/commit/push`. A leadership restructure proposal was built for Rocky and Anthony, formalizing Daniel as COO and repositioning Kyle to VP of Business Development, with a full SVG org chart and first-person business case PDF. A comprehensive QC implementation package was produced in response to a powder coat incident, including a 13-slide PowerPoint, defect tracking Excel, corrective action policy, and training sign-off documents. The Laguna Swift CNC SOP has gone through multiple revisions (now Rev 2.0), with a merged vendor manual, embedded photos, and a standalone Homing Fault Diagnosis Guide (MR-CNC-DIAG-001). CNC/CAM work in Fusion 360 has been ongoing, including CadQuery scripts for post variants (P-CBL-SM-LINE-42, BASE-42-6, BASE-42-8), shop floor instruction sheets, and fixture design. A 5S/Lean Six Sigma policy document (MR-OPS-001) was created for shop floor standards. Customer-facing purchase agreement templates (5-page, 14-section) were built for the sales team. A Dell Precision 7680 laptop (former company-issued) was wiped and restored via bootable USB on a MacBook Air M2.

*Earlier context*

Daniel led a large-scale materials tracking and ordering workbook project (Maisy_Railing_Materials_ALL_ORDERS.xlsx) consolidating 37+ customer and wholesale order sheets with inventory, BOM, and consolidated ordering logic. A companion HTML app (MaisyRailing_WorkbookGenerator.html) was built for AI-assisted order extraction. The GODMODE Excel master file (101 tabs, MaisyRailing_GODMODE_v2_0_12.xlsm) was split into 8 themed workbooks with branded dashboards, and a SheetPilot Excel Task Pane Add-in was partially built. A 12-document manufacturing automation proposal package was completed, including per-station ROI analyses, facility layout, software/licensing requirements, and a Microsoft 365 migration proposal — all delivered to Rocky and Anthony. A process cost analysis workbook was rebuilt from per-section to per-piece model with a product selector driving all calculations, using floor-measured production data. A browser-based 2D/3D railing configurator (MaisyRailing_Designer.html) was built with BOM engine, pricing, and a 34-page technical/business documentation PDF. Production Queue Analyzer and Hot/Rush Order Tracker HTML apps were built for shop floor use. Hiring documentation for 7 positions was created, including interview forms and a full onboarding package. A 3D printing capability proposal and aluminum privacy panel proposal were produced for ownership review.

*Long-term background*

Daniel joined Maisy Railing with a defined scope to build operational infrastructure from scratch and execute a full automation transformation. Early work included building the foundational operations documentation suite (8 SOPs, KPI dashboard, time study workbook with industry benchmarks), a comprehensive shop reference guide (Maisy_08_Shop_Reference.xlsx, grown to 18+ tabs), vendor pricing and inventory tracking systems, a sales order workbook redesign, and an EOS implementation playbook tailored to Maisy Railing. He also produced early hiring materials, delivery cost analyses, expense policy proposals, and customer-facing installation guides for cable and glass railing systems. His MacBook Air M2 is his primary machine; work email runs through Apple Mail (not Gmail).

---

**Standing instructions**

- Always write documents from Daniel's first-person perspective, as if he authored them himself.
- Email signature format: "Daniel Jones, Director of Operations, Maisy Railing, 208.603.8149" with the Maisy Railing logo linked to https://www.maisyrailing.com/.
- Always update the Instructions tab when modifying any spreadsheet or form. If no Instructions tab exists, create one. Write instructions clearly for non-technical users.
- When creating or editing any document: always update revision numbers in the header and/or footer AND in the revision history table. If no revision table exists, always create one. Increment the rev number logically based on the change made.
- Daniel is planning to request a compensation proposal document. Fair market salary range: $115K–$145K base, plus performance bonuses tied to automation milestones.
- Daniel lives in Sandpoint, ID (not Hayden, ID).
- Every document generated for Maisy Railing should automatically include a companion one-page executive summary formatted for owner-level review.
